pairs.question: When and where was Andrew David Greasley born?
pairs.answer: Andrew David Greasley was born on 23 March 1960 in Leicester, England.

pairs.question: What were Andrew David Greasley's cricketing roles in the team?
pairs.answer: Greasley was a right-handed batsman and a right-arm off-break bowler.

pairs.question: Which teams did Andrew David Greasley play for during his cricket career?
pairs.answer: He played for Leicestershire Second XI (1979–1983), British Colleges, Cheshire (1990–1997), Leicester Ivanhoe, York CC, and Marple Cricket Club.

pairs.question: What significant achievement did Andrew David Greasley have with Cheshire in 1996?
pairs.answer: He was part of the Cheshire team that won the MCC Trophy in 1996, defeating Bedfordshire in the Lords final. Greasley took two wickets in that match.

pairs.question: What was Andrew David Greasley's performance in his List A cricket appearances?
pairs.answer: Greasley made two List A appearances in the NatWest Trophy (1995–1996). In his first match, he scored 2 runs batting at the tailend, and in his second match, he scored a duck (0 runs).

pairs.question: In which year did Andrew David Greasley win the Yorkshire League with York CC?
pairs.answer: He was an important member of the 1988 Yorkshire League winning team while playing for York CC.

pairs.question: What administrative role did Andrew David Greasley hold at York CC?
pairs.answer: He served as the Club Secretary for York CC during his time there.

pairs.question: At what age and for which club was Andrew David Greasley still playing cricket in 2018?
pairs.answer: In 2018, at the age of 58, Greasley was still playing for Marple Cricket Club in the Cheshire County Premier Division.
